summary: The portfolio tiered down: 1 healthy / 3 needs-attention / 1 at-risk / 1 stalled, off last week's 3 healthy / 3 needs-attention / 0 at-risk. The defining story is the host-side commit stall: five of seven repos are HEAD-frozen six to nine days, every HIGH on HBH, Scheduler, and YaB is correct file work that the attended release.ps1 never shipped, and committed-ready queue items grew from 13 to 21. HBH tipped to at-risk on a second HIGH (a corrupt tracked git object, FUSE damage reaching the object store) and YaA tipped to stalled on its tenth unactioned cut-v1.0 review. BR is the bright spot: a 57-commit single-day spike unfroze its tree and landed a month of deferred best-practices work.

topActions.label: Run one attended Windows release pass to drain the 21 committed-ready items and unfreeze five repos; start Scheduler, then YaB, then HBH
Repair HBH's corrupt loose object d188556 from origin and promote git-guard.ps1 to a shared helper wired into every release.ps1
Build the preship gate owed since 06-08: refuse a pill bump with no matching changelog footer entry, plus a bare-auto-message guard
Land or revert v0.98.10 (eighth cycle) and run the deferred footprint defense-balance review
Ship in the self-non-polluting order: gitignore *.bak, git rm --cached the two tracked .bak, delete the untracked, then release
release.ps1 -Bump none to clear the dead index.lock via Assert-GitSafe and land five doc edits; do not hand-delete the lock
Cut v1.0, mark 'Symbol + label polish' done, and drop Apothecary to weekly review (tenth carry)
Move YaC and YaA to weekly review and pause bar-raise-scheduler-daily until HEAD moves
Make the next BR session an M1 mechanics row (stuck at 40% for 12 days) and land the release-footer/auto-msg gate
Normalize the queue to one project ID per project (BR rising/br/brackish, YaA apothecary/yaa/yaapothecary, HBH hbh/htbh)
